title: Free hand messaging and ink cluster editor
abstract: A computer-implemented method and system for providing users with free-hand writing in a user specific language. The computer- implemented method includes capturing a plurality of individual ink strokes created by a user within a specified amount of time through one of a touch and gesture based interface on an Ink Cluster Editor that is integrated with a messaging application, wherein the messaging application allows the user to send and receive handwritten messages including non-roman letter based letters. The computer-implemented method also includes editing, formatting and manipulating the Ink Cluster represented as bitmap cluster converted to suitable vector and image file formats used in external applications. The computer-implemented method includes converting the Ink Clusters into a desired format and recognizing by a handwriting recognition engine to generate corresponding text. The computer-implemented method includes exporting the generated text along with formatting information to one or more external applications through a user interface.
claims:
Claims STATEMENT OF CLAIMS 1. A computer-implemented method for providing users to free-hand writing in a user specific language, the computer- implemented method comprises: capturing a plurality of individual ink strokes created by a user within a specified amount of time through one of a touch and gesture based interface on an Ink Cluster Editor that is integrated with a messaging application, the messaging application allows the user to send and receive handwritten messages including non-roman letter based letters; storing the ink strokes as vectors with coordinates, boundaries, curves and segment lines; grouping the individual ink strokes as a single Ink Cluster, wherein the Ink Cluster represents a handwriting in a user specific language; storing the Ink Cluster as vector data along with a corresponding bitmap cluster; editing, formatting and manipulating the Ink Cluster represented as the bitmap cluster converted to suitable vector and image file formats used in external applications; converting the Ink Clusters into a desired format and recognizing by a handwriting recognition engine to generate corresponding text; and exporting the generated text along with formatting information to one or more external applications through a user interface. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the ink strokes are atleast one of a single ink stroke, multiple ink strokes, single or multiple words, drawings and diagrams that the user creates. 3. The computer- implemented method of claim 1 wherein the ink strokes are independent of languages, skew, size, color, shape and number of written rows. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the grouping of individual ink strokes further comprises: transferring the Ink Cluster to a card section of the user interface in response to a trigger, wherein the trigger occurs based on a user action. 5. The computer- implemented method of claim 1 and further comprising: 

 scaling the Ink Cluster to a row size as set by the user in the settings and contents aspect ratio is maintained, wherein the row size determines the size of the final writing irrespective of the size of writing on the canvas section of the user interface. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 and further comprising: displaying the bitmap cluster; and allowing the user to continue writing and drawing on the canvas section of the user interface. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein a space is introduced between each Ink Cluster through one of specified by the user and automatic insertion. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein formatting the Ink Cluster further comprises: storing formatting information with the Ink Cluster and Bitmap Cluster. 9. A computer program product stored on a non-transitory computer-readable medium that when executed by a processor, performs a method for providing users to free-hand writing in a user specific language, the computer program product comprises: capturing a plurality of individual ink strokes created by a user within a specified amount of time through one of a touch and gesture based interface on an Ink Cluster Editor that is integrated with a messaging application, the messaging application allows the user to send and receive handwritten messages including non-roman letter based letters; storing the ink strokes as vectors with coordinates, boundaries, curves and segment lines; grouping the individual ink strokes as a single Ink Cluster, wherein the Ink Cluster represents a handwriting in a user specific language; storing the Ink Cluster as vector data along with a corresponding bitmap cluster; editing, formatting and manipulating the Ink Cluster represented as the bitmap cluster converted to suitable vector and image file formats used in external applications; converting the Ink Clusters into a desired format and recognizing by a handwriting recognition engine to generate corresponding text; and 

 exporting the generated text along with formatting information to one or more external applications through a user interface. 10. The computer program product of claim 9 wherein the ink strokes are atleast one of a single ink stroke, multiple ink strokes, single or multiple words, drawings and diagrams that the user creates. 11. The computer program product of claim 9 wherein the ink strokes are independent of languages, skew, size, color, shape and number of written rows. 12. The computer program product of claim 9 wherein the grouping of individual ink strokes further comprises: transferring the Ink Cluster to a card section of the user interface in response to a trigger, wherein the trigger occurs based on a user action. 13. The computer program product of claim 9 and further comprising: scaling the Ink Cluster to a row size as set by the user in the settings and contents aspect ratio is maintained, wherein the row size determines the size of the final writing irrespective of the size of writing on the canvas of the user interface. 14. The computer program product of claim 9 and further comprising: displaying the bitmap cluster; and allowing the user to continue writing and drawing on the canvas. 15. The computer program product of claim 9 wherein a space is introduced between each Ink Cluster through one of specified by the user and automatic insertion. 16. The computer program product of claim 9 wherein formatting the Ink Cluster further comprises: storing formatting information with the Ink Cluster and Bitmap Cluster. 17. A system for providing users to free-hand writing in a user specific language, the system comprises: a computing device operated by a user, wherein the computing device is a touch based device; one or more receiving devices operated by other users who receive messages sent by the user of the computing device; 

 a network; a server; a database to store Ink Clusters, Bitmap Clusters and formatting information created by the user in complex foreign languages; and a processor configured within the computing device and operable to perform: capture a plurality of individual ink strokes created by a user within a specified amount of time through one of a touch and gesture based interface on an Ink Cluster Editor that is integrated with a messaging application, wherein the messaging application allows the user to send and receive handwritten messages including non-roman letter based letters; store the ink strokes as vectors with coordinates, boundaries, curves and segment lines; group the individual ink strokes as a single Ink Cluster, wherein the Ink Cluster represents a handwriting in a user specific language; store the Ink Cluster as vector data along with a corresponding bitmap cluster; edit, format and manipulate the Ink Cluster represented as bitmap cluster converted to suitable vector and image file formats used in external applications; convert the Ink Clusters into a desired format and recognizing by a handwriting recognition engine to generate corresponding text; export the generated text along with formatting information to one or more external applications through a user interface. 18. The system of claim 17 wherein the computing device further comprises: one or more external applications; an Ink Cluster Editor to allow the user to free-hand write text, symbols, draw pictures, drawings and edit through a user interface of the computing device; and a handwriting recognition engine generates text from the output. 19. The system of claim 18 wherein the Ink Cluster Editor further comprises: 

 a canvas is a larger section of the user interface that allows the user to write and draw using a writing instrument in user specific languages; a card is a smaller section of the user interface and responsible for Ink Cluster and Bitmap Cluster information, storage and display; interactive buttons to enable the user to edit and format the Ink Clusters and Bitmap Clusters; and settings to allow the user to set a fixed height and width of rows, ink strokes, color, thickness and spacing between Ink Clusters. 20. The system of claim 19 wherein the Interactive Buttons further comprises: editing tools allow the user to edit the free-hand writing created as Ink Clusters; and formatting tools allow the users to Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough and Align the Bitmap Clusters and Ink Clusters created from free-hand writing.
